---
title: "Introduction to R and RStudio"
subtitle: "DMgtAsia · Data Science Foundations Module"
author:
  - name: "Dr. Arun Mitra"
    affiliation: "AIIMS Bibinagar"
  - name: "Prof. (Dr.) Biju Soman"
    affiliation: "AMCHSS, SCTIMST, Trivandrum"
date: last-modified
---

![](dmgt_asia_logo.png){width=160 fig-align="left"}

## Welcome

This is the **data science foundations** module of the DMgtAsia course, and the precursor to
the Spatial Epidemiology module. It introduces R and RStudio for public health work, from first
principles to a reproducible analysis.

No prior R experience is assumed. The first thing you run will work.

## What you will be able to do

- Explain why R and a reproducible workflow suit public health.
- Find your way around RStudio: panes, Projects, and `here()`.
- Use R basics: objects, vectors, data types, functions, and packages.
- Read a dataset, inspect it, and wrangle it with the tidyverse.
- Follow good data science practice: reproducibility, project structure, and coding style.

## Sessions

| Session | Theme | Focus |
|------|-------|-------|
| **Session-I** | Foundations | R, RStudio, the data science workflow, R basics |
| **Session-II** | Best Practices in Data Science | reproducibility, tool choice, literate programming, structure, style |
| **Session-III** | Working with Data | exploratory data analysis and the `dplyr` verbs |
| **Session-IV** | Exercises | hands-on practice on a real dataset |

Start with **[Setup](setup.qmd)** to prepare your machine, then open Session-I.

## Live code

The Foundations session runs R in your browser (webR), so you can edit and run code on the page
with nothing installed. For the full hands-on work you use R and RStudio on your own machine.

## Acknowledgements

Developed at AIIMS Bibinagar and the Achutha Menon Centre for Health Science Studies, SCTIMST.
Built on R, the tidyverse, and Quarto.
